titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Laura McPhee Photographs of the American West
Guide to the McPhee (Laura) Photographs of the American West
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 7, 2021 at 03:03

did.unittitle: Laura McPhee Photographs of the American West

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photographs created by Laura McPhee that document sites in the American West, 2008-2013, including landscapes and communities in Arizona, Idaho, Nevada, Utah, and Wyoming.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Photographs created by Laura McPhee that document sites in the American West, 2008-2013, including landscapes and communities in Arizona, Idaho, Nevada, Utah, and Wyoming. The collection consists of 23 chromogenic color prints, 44 x 56 cm, including two pairs of prints presented as diptychs, as well as a single print measuring 51 x 61 cm. The collection also includes a portrait of her daughter, Isobel McPhee, created in Nevada.

Photographs of Arizona in 2012 include views of the former open pit copper and turquoise mine operated by the Phelps Dodge Corporation at Lavender Pit in Bisbee and a view of the Ray Mine Project, a copper mine in Pinal County.

Photographs of Idaho, 2007-2013, include discrete views of the Silver City and the Meadow Creek Wind Farm in Ririe as well as sites along the Big Wood River and Salmon River. Two prints document the aftermath of a forest fire at the White Cloud Peaks.
A diptych of photographs of Nevada in 2012 documents the Smoke Creek Desert with the railway of the Southern Pacific Railroad Company.

Photographs of Utah, 2010-2011, include discrete views of Burr Creek in Long Canyon and Lime Creek in Valley of the Gods as well as views of electric lines along the Book Cliffs, the interior of a store in the ghost town of Mutual, and tanker railcars on a railroad siding at the Moab Uranium Mill Tailings Remedial Action (UMTRA) project site in Crescent Junction.

Photographs of Wyoming in 2013 include views of natural gas wells in the Wind River Basin, an abandoned automobile service station in Green River, and the Kemmerer Mine coal strip mining complex in Lincoln County as well as images of Scotty Lake in Sweetwater County and the Worthen Meadow Reservoir in Shoshone National Forest.
Manuscript inscriptions of titles and dates by the photographer on versos of most prints.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Laura McPhee on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2014 and 2017, and gift of Laura McPhee, 2014 and 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Photographs of Arizona, 2012. II. Photographs of Idaho, 2007-2013. III. Photographs of Nevada, 2012. IV. Photographs of Utah, 2010-2011. V. Photographs of Wyoming, 2013.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Laura McPhee River of No Return Collection (WA Photos 591). Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Laura McPhee (born 1958)
bioghist.p: Laura McPhee is a photographer. Born in New York City and raised in central New Jersey, she earned a Bachelor of Arts in Art History from Princeton University in 1980, and a Master of Fine Arts in Photography from the Rhode Island School of Design in 1986. Her work, which ranges from portrait to landscape to still life, is widely exhibited nationally and internationally. She has been a member of the faculty at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design since 1986 and lives in Brookline, Massachusetts.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Laura McPhee Photographs of the American West. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Arizona
Former Copper and Turquoise Mine, Lavender Pit, Bisbee, Arizona (Negative number 94829)
Lavender Pit, Closed Copper and Turquoise Mine, Bisbee, Arizona (Negative number 94826)
Tailings from the Ray Cooper Mine in the Sonoran Desert near Florence, Arizona (Negative number 94824)
Photographs of Idaho
Big Wood River, Blaine County, Idaho (Negative number 94853)
East Fork, Salmon River, Custer County, Idaho (Negative number 94699)
Late Summer (Drifting Fireweed) (Unidentified negative number)
Meadow Creek Wind Farm, Ririe, Idaho (Negative number 95049)
Salmon River, Clayton, Idaho (Negative number 94734)
Silver City, Owyhee County, Idaho (Negative number 94785)
Untitled (Unidentified negative number)

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Laura McPhee on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image documents a meadow with automobile tracks made in the foilage.

did.unittitle: Untitled (Unidentified negative number)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a snow-covered Washington Peak through a fire-scarred forest at the White Cloud Peaks.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Nevada
Isobel (Unidentified negative number)
Smoke Creek Desert and the Southern Pacific Rail Lines, Nevada (Negative number 94839 and 94846)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diptych of 2 photographic prints, each measuring 44 x 56 cm

did.unittitle: Photographs of Utah
Cottonwoods, Lime Creek, Valley of the Gods, Utah (Negative number 94638)
High Voltage Transmission Lines by the Book Cliffs, near Green River, Utah (Negative number 94664)
Long Canyon and Burr Creek, Escalante, Utah (Negative numbers 94554 and 94537)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diptych of 2 photographic prints, each measuring 44 x 56 cm, that forms a panoramic view.

did.unittitle: Mutual Store, Spring Canyon Road, Near Helper, Utah (Negative number 94643)
Tanker Train, Moab Uranium Mill Tailings Disposal Site, Crescent Junction, Utah (Negative number 94684)
Photographs of Wyoming
The Mesa, Natural Gas Wells near the Wind River Range, Wyoming (Negative number 95040)
Old Highway 80, Green River, Wyoming (Negative number 95007
Open Pit Coal Mine, Kemmerer, Wyoming (Negative number 95014)
Rain, Worthen Meadow Reservoir, Wind River Range, Wyoming (Negative number 95034)
Scolty Lake, Sweetwater County, Wyoming (Negative number 95026)
